bullets.text: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, according to Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu, as Europe experiences a severe heatwave.
A UN commission of inquiry has accused Israel of committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children; Israel has rejected the report as a "libellous sham."
Kenya's health minister has ordered a halt to the construction of a US-run Ebola facility, following a court ruling to stop the work.
A Gold Coast company is set to launch hypersonic rockets, capable of reaching seven times the speed of sound, to test Defence and science equipment under extreme conditions.

Concerns have been raised in Tasmania regarding the release of over half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage into waterways, including popular swimming spots, during the 2024 to 2025 financial year.

Experts are questioning why a triple murderer was granted bail before a deadly shooting spree in Lake Cargelligo, which resulted in the deaths of three people, including Sophie Quinn who had previously sought police protection from her former partner.
US chipmakers, including Nvidia, led a Wall Street slide as the Nasdaq declined for a second day amid investor worries over potential interest rate hikes.
Emerging-market assets experienced a broad decline as the dollar strengthened to its highest level of the year, compounded by a significant selloff in South Korean technology shares.
A $7 billion private credit fund managed by Morgan Stanley has capped investor withdrawals at 5%, fulfilling less than half of the redemption requests made in the second quarter.
